commit.message:
fix:devspace broker hook race (#1483)

## Summary

During `devspace dev`, the ark-broker experiences two intermittent
failure modes:
   
- **Startup race condition**: The broker's Helm chart includes a
post-install hook that restarts the ark-controller to discover the
broker's OTEL tracing endpoint. DevSpace runs `start_dev` immediately
after `create_deployments` without waiting for this hook to complete.
When the controller restart overlaps with DevSpace's pod attachment, the
broker pod gets disrupted and DevSpace exits with "lost
connection to pod". This is timing-dependent — fast clusters often avoid
it, slower or loaded clusters hit it consistently.

- **Mid-session OOM crash (~20 min)**: The broker stores messages,
stream chunks, OTEL spans, and events in unbounded in-memory arrays. The
`maxItems` limits defined in `values.yaml` are only enforced
during `save()`, which runs on graceful shutdown (`SIGTERM`). During a
dev session, these arrays grow indefinitely. Once the Node.js process
exceeds the container's 1024MB memory limit, Kubernetes sends
`SIGKILL` (bypassing graceful shutdown entirely), the pod dies, and
DevSpace loses its connection.

  ### Fix
- **DevSpace pipeline** (`services/ark-broker/devspace.yaml`): Added
`kubectl wait` and `kubectl rollout status` steps between
`create_deployments` and `start_dev` to ensure the Helm hook Job
completes
and the ark-controller stabilizes before DevSpace attaches to the broker
pod
- **In-memory trimming**
(`services/ark-broker/ark-broker/src/broker-item-stream.ts`): Enforce
`maxItems` limit in `BrokerItemStream.append()` so oldest items are
evicted at insertion time, preventing
  unbounded memory growth regardless of session duration
  
## Checklist

- [ ] Follow the [contributor guide](./CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [ ] End-to-end tests pass
- [ ] Unit tests for essential parts of your code, e.g. APIs exposed via
SDKs or endpoints
- [ ] Update `./docs`
- [ ] Recognize contributors with "@all-contributors please add <person>
for <code, bug, docs, etc>"
- [ ] Linked issues #eg101

commit.message:
Uptaking the refactored Power BI synch engine for the OOB apps (#6763)

#### Summary

- Introduce the `PBI Report Setup` enum and interface to represent Power
BI report app configurations, replacing raw field IDs with
strongly-typed enum values
- Refactor all embedded report pages (~120) to use the new
`OpenPowerBIEmbeddedReportPageValidation` procedure, which consolidates
`EnsureUserAcceptedPowerBITerms` + `GetReportIdAndEnsureSetup` +
deployment-in-progress prompts into a single call
- Adds a new wizard experience for deploying reports when selecting an
unconfigured embedded report page (for evaluation companies)
- Add `FindReportSetup` to resolve a `Deployable Report` type back to
its `PBI Report Setup` interface, eliminating repeated ordinal iteration
in subscribers and the deployments page extension
- Fix a pre-existing bug where the ABC Analysis page (Inventory) was
incorrectly referencing the Sales report ID

* fix: set GOMEMLIMIT and gracefully handle proxy errors under memory pressure

Without GOMEMLIMIT the Go GC follows default behaviour and the heap can
grow past the cgroup limit before collection runs, causing severe memory
pressure on constrained runners.  Setting debug.SetMemoryLimit in the
memoryguard makes the GC aggressive at the configured threshold.

The proxy error handlers for request-write, response-read, and
response-write only allowed a fixed set of "expected close" errors
through when pressureCloseMode was active.  Under cgroup memory
pressure the kernel can surface other error types (timeouts, socket
errors) that fell through to the ERROR log and silently dropped
connections.  Now any I/O error is treated as expected when
isIngressRecordingPaused() is true.

commit.message: docs: performance audit report\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: detect empty GitHub releases and propagate exit code on failure (#57)

* chore: update DotnetPackaging packages to 10.1.7

Update all DotnetPackaging package references from 10.1.0 to 10.1.7.
Flatpak stays at 9.0.6 (removed from DotnetPackaging in v10.0.0).

Notable upstream changes in 10.1.1–10.1.7:
- Improved error handling in DotnetPublisher (better build error extraction)
- RPM packaging fixes (AppStream metadata, URI parsing, header structure)
- IsTerminal changed to Maybe<bool> (inferred from OutputType)
- systemd service/daemon support for .deb packages
- from-directory subcommand in CLI tool

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: detect empty GitHub releases and propagate exit code on failure

When all package builds fail, the deployer now:
- Deletes the empty release instead of leaving it on GitHub
- Cleans up the release if an exception occurs during upload
- Returns a non-zero exit code so CI pipelines fail correctly

The exit code bug was caused by SetAction(Func<ParseResult, Task>) which
ignores Environment.ExitCode. Now uses a closure variable returned from Main.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: fast duplicate scan for Overview using partial hashing

Overview (Smart Care) now calls findDuplicates(quickScan: true)
which hashes only the first 4KB of each file instead of the
entire contents. This dramatically reduces I/O for the overview
scan while keeping the full accurate scan for the Duplicates
module. Also skips files under 4KB in quick mode.

commit.message:
feat: WebSocket, graceful shutdown, upstream timeouts, replace-file, replacement diff logging (v1.3.0)

Five improvements:

1. WebSocket transparent proxying
   Set FlushInterval=-1 on httputil.ReverseProxy. Go 1.20+ automatically
   handles 101 Switching Protocols and enters bidirectional copy mode,
   bypassing ModifyResponse (correct — WS frames cannot be string-replaced
   without corrupting the framing protocol). Logged as 'WS\u2191'.

2. Graceful shutdown (-drain, default 15s)
   Switched from http.ListenAndServe to http.Server so we can call
   Shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM. In-flight requests are drained for up
   to -drain seconds before the process exits. ReadHeaderTimeout=30s
   added as Slowloris protection.

3. Upstream timeouts (-timeout, default 30s)
   Applied to transport.DialContext, TLSHandshakeTimeout, and
   ResponseHeaderTimeout independently. Prevents hung upstreams from
   leaking goroutines. Set 0 to disable.

4. -replace-file flag
   Load replacement pairs from a text file: one original:alias per line,
   # comments (full-line and inline), blank lines ignored. Combined with
   -replace pairs (CLI pairs win on conflict). Avoids shell quoting
   headaches for large pair sets.

5. Replacement count logging
   ToAliasDiff/ToOriginalDiff added to Replacer. Every request and
   response log line now shows '[N replaced]' when user replacements
   fired. Zero overhead when no replacements occur (early return).
   Verbose mode continues to show full headers and body preview.

Tests: 26 pass (4 new: TestUpstreamTimeout, TestReplaceDiff,
TestLoadReplaceFile, TestLoadReplaceFileInvalidLine)

commit.message:
Replace JDK glob matcher with Ant/Maven-style path matching via plexus-utils (#58)

* feat: replace JDK glob matcher with Ant-style path matching via plexus-utils

Replace FileSystems.getDefault().getPathMatcher("glob:...") with
AntStylePathMatcher backed by Plexus SelectorUtils.matchPath().

This fixes the ** (double star) behavior: patterns like **/*.java
now correctly match files in the base directory (zero intermediate
directories), following the Ant/Maven convention where ** matches
zero or more directories.

Previously, JDK's glob required at least one directory for **/ patterns.
